summary.intent: Address 8 unresolved Codex review findings on PR #710 for the GridList component in the ars-ui repository, covering behavioral correctness issues in keyboard interaction, focus management, and action observability.
summary.outcome: All 8 Codex findings were fixed (disabled sync focus clearing, composite tab stops, Space toggle, Shift+Arrow range selection, RTL arrows, empty-range guard, item action observability, monotonic timestamp fallback), unit tests and regressions added, spec updated, and changes committed as 'Address GridList Codex review findings'.

repo: Codex review flow: apply 'snapshot-reviewed' label + post '@codex review' comment after each push, then poll reactions (👀 = reviewing, 👍 = approved) and GraphQL reviewThreads for unresolved Codex threads

Callback/effect pattern: item actions are not stored directly in state but emitted as adapter-drained effects, matching the TransitionResult/Effect lifecycle used across action_group and other components
RTL arrow direction is resolved via Direction::from(locale.direction()), consistent with TagGroup and Table components
Controlled-state pattern for new observable fields uses 'requested_*_key' naming (e.g. requested_action_key), matching existing requested_selected_keys surface
INSTA_UPDATE=always flag is used when intentional snapshot changes are expected (e.g. composite tab-index changes)
Spec files live in spec/components/, spec/leptos-components/, and spec/dioxus-components/ and must be kept in sync with core contract changes
cargo xtask spec validate is the canonical spec conformance check command
Env struct does not carry platform effects, so GridList core cannot call a platform clock directly — convenience helpers use synthetic advancing timestamps instead

code.finding: Disabled sync focus clearing, composite tab stop logic, and Space toggle behavior were missing or incorrect in this region
Shift+Arrow range selection and RTL arrow handling required fixes here
Empty-range guard was missing in selection logic
Item action observability (requested_action_key field + accessor) and monotonic timestamp fallback for convenience keydown helper were added here

workflow: Action callback closures in tests require Send/Sync — use Arc<Mutex<_>> for shared test state, not plain Rc/RefCell
apply_patch in large multi-hunk context can fail silently if context lines don't match exactly — split large patches into smaller focused chunks
Test expectations for effect-based callbacks must drain the effect queue (run pending effects) rather than checking side effects synchronously after the state transition
cargo +nightly fmt --check before commit; apply with cargo +nightly fmt when violations found
Focused verification sequence: fmt → unit tests → spec conformance → wasm compile check → proptest → insta → xtask spec validate

friction: Large apply_patch call failed due to context line mismatch, requiring the patch to be split into smaller chunks
Closure type inference error in action effect test required explicit parameter type annotations
Shift+Arrow RTL test had wrong starting row — needed a row where the reversed target is enabled
Action test initially modeled callbacks as direct side effects rather than adapter-drained effects, requiring test restructure
Codex review cycle required ~10-20 minutes of polling with multiple 120-second sleeps before the verdict arrived
Spec file was already staged while code/snapshot edits were unstaged, requiring staging normalization before commit
open_items: Codex dropped 👀 without 👍 — the commit was pushed but it's unclear if Codex will re-review the new head automatically or if another '@codex review' trigger is needed

The convenience keydown timestamp uses a synthetic advancing timestamp rather than a real platform monotonic clock — this is a known limitation of Env not carrying platform effects, worth revisiting if platform clock access is added to Env

spec/leptos-components/data-display/grid-list.md and spec/dioxus-components/data-display/grid-list.md were checked for relevant terms but may need updates if the framework-specific specs reference the changed core contracts
